findings.label: Hero does not name the operational buyer
findings.issue: The hero promise is broad and does not name the operations buyer, handoff risk, or weekly review trigger from the source packet.
findings.suggestedFix: Rewrite the hero around operations leaders who need owner-visible handoffs before customer commitments slip.
findings.proofNeeded: No proof needed; this is a message-alignment rewrite.
findings.agentInstruction: Rewrite the hero with the operations buyer, handoff-risk language, and a concrete review-loop outcome.

findings.label: Time-savings claim needs approved proof
findings.quotedText: Teams save hours every week with automated handoffs.
findings.issue: The claim implies a quantified productivity outcome, but the demo proof library only approves qualitative workflow visibility.
findings.suggestedFix: Either add an approved study for the time-savings claim or soften the sentence to a qualitative workflow-visibility benefit.
findings.proofNeeded: Approved study or customer quote that supports the time-savings claim.
findings.agentInstruction: Do not preserve or strengthen the time-savings claim without approved proof.

findings.label: Absolute guarantee should be softened
findings.issue: The absolute promise overstates control and can create proof and support expectations the source packet does not authorize.
findings.suggestedFix: Change the line to a risk-reduction claim such as "Spot ownership gaps before customer commitments slip."
findings.proofNeeded: Approved support or customer-commitment proof before using absolute language.
findings.agentInstruction: Soften the absolute guarantee into risk-reduction language.

openQuestions.proofNeeded: Approved study or customer quote that supports the time-savings claim.
openQuestions.agentInstruction: Do not preserve or strengthen the time-savings claim without approved proof.

publicClaimFlags.riskNotes: Quantified productivity outcomes need approved methodology before public use.
